notes: Solid pong implementation with AI mode, pause, and win detection, but player 1 controls conflict (ArrowUp/Down mapped to both players in single-player mode) and the dt variable is computed but unused in physics making movement frame-rate dependent.

Looks great and single-player works, but 2-player mode is broken due to shared Arrow key mappings, the game speed is frame-rate dependent, and pressing Escape on the Game Over screen bugs out into a Paused state instead of returning to the title.

Looks like Pong and runs, but controls are badly conflicted (P1 also uses Arrow keys while 2P mode assigns Arrow keys to P2), pause/escape logic is inconsistent with on-screen prompts, and overall gameplay fairness suffers.
